mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and real GDP confirms robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return over 60 days in > 40% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation remains persistently above 3.0 percent, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy, which suppresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive in > 60% of high-inflation periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when inflation exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in > 50% of observations, or the rank correlation with 5-day returns falls below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent indicates a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when housing inventory levels are below historical averages.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in > 45% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: A Federal Reserve rate cut exceeding 25 basis points signals a pivot to accommodative policy, which lowers borrowing costs and equity discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases by > 0.25% in a single meeting
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 50% of rate-cut instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance during periods of stagflation.
